authoritative_sources.title: Producer Portal
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAG-AFTRA's official producer portal is an authoritative guild source for production-facing paperwork, signatory obligations, and performer-document workflow references used by studio operations teams.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Talent and performer paperwork grounding before day-of-production execution
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the pack to request the governing performer paperwork and signatory context before treating talent movement, check-in, or closeout as complete.

authoritative_sources.title: Production Time Report (Exhibit G)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The official SAG-AFTRA Exhibit G form is an authoritative industry artifact for documenting performer work time, meal periods, overtime, and production-day evidence that studio operations cannot ignore at closeout.
used_for: refusal to mark talent-support work complete without labor-facing records
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Drives the requirement that completion or fulfillment records stay blocked when performer time-report evidence or equivalent tenant-mapped labor artifacts are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Reports Compliance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DGA's official reports-compliance department page is authoritative for contractually required production documents such as deal memos, post-production addenda, employment data reports, and weekly work lists that shape studio staffing and documentation flow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Staffing-document validation and labor-facing record handoff
used_for: handoff boundaries around staffing and compliance records
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating staffing and crew-support closeout as generic admin work when contractually required DGA-facing records are part of the execution package.

authoritative_sources.title: Locations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Film Commission is an official state production authority, and its locations guidance is a recognized workflow reference for studio zones, permit pathways, insurance requirements, and state-facility filming dependencies.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Anchors the stage and venue readiness model so the lane asks for the booked facility, permit path, and insurance dependencies before advancing work.

authoritative_sources.title: Permit FAQ
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Film Commission's permit FAQ is an official workflow reference for who needs a permit, insurance, film-monitor costs, and special-effects permitting on state property.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Venue dependency and permit exception handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds concrete refusal triggers for proceeding with venue-impact work when permit, insurance, or special-effects dependencies are incomplete.

authoritative_sources.title: Safety in Motion Picture Productions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Film Commission's safety page is an official workflow reference for safety-advisor programs, risk assessments, and final safety evaluation reporting tied directly to production execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Production-risk review and final safety evaluation handling
used_for: escalation when safety-review ownership is required
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps higher-risk soundstage or venue work from being treated as ordinary studio support when safety-advisor review or evaluation reporting is required.

authoritative_sources.title: Safety Bulletins
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CSATF safety bulletins are recognized industry control references for motion picture and television productions and explicitly govern bulletin distribution, call-sheet attachment, and special procedures such as minors performing physical activities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Call-sheet safety attachment, day-of-work controls, and special-case escalation
used_for: special-procedure triggers for minors or physical activity
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Provides the control vocabulary for safety-bulletin distribution and prevents the lane from closing work when required call-sheet safety materials are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: NFPA 140 Standard Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NFPA 140 is the recognized soundstage and production-location fire-safety standard for motion picture and television production facilities, providing the public control baseline for studio and stage safety boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Soundstage readiness and fire-life-safety boundary handling
used_for: adjacent-lane routing to fire-life-safety and engineering owners
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to escalate when soundstage readiness depends on facility-safety approval instead of routine coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: Entertainment Work Permit for Minors
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Industrial Relations, Division of Labor Standards Enforcement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California DLSE is the official labor authority for entertainment work permits for minors, which materially changes whether studio operations may proceed with youth performers or participants.
used_for: routing to labor or studio-teacher ownership
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds a material industry-specific hard stop for minors so the lane blocks progression instead of treating missing permit evidence as an ordinary admin gap.

workflow_stages.name: production and venue grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the production, shoot date, unit, stage or venue, current lifecycle state, and the governing call-sheet, schedule, permit, and audience or talent context before work advances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: studio, audience, and talent readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify stage bookings, venue approvals, permits, insurance, audience rosters, cast lists, release dependencies, and staffing paperwork before day-of-production work moves to ready or in-progress.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: day-of-production execution and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track call-sheet execution, check-in and holding workflows, safety bulletin distribution, minor or protected-participant controls, and rework when schedule, venue, or labor evidence conflicts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence capture and wrap
workflow_stages.description: Assemble daily production reports, time reports, releases, wrap checklists, and next-owner notes before marking work completed or handed off.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: rights, labor, safety, and venue-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when legal clearance, union interpretation, payroll signoff, fire-life-safety approval, engineering, or special-effects authority materially changes the safe next action.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: call sheets, one-line schedules, production calendars, and stage-booking calendars
cast lists, audience manifests, holding lists, greenroom assignments, contact sheets, and transport or parking plans
deal memos, appearance releases, audience waivers, Exhibit G time reports, weekly work lists, and other labor-facing paperwork
state or local filming permits, certificates of insurance, special-effects or drone approvals, venue rules, and facilities notices
safety bulletins, risk assessments, final safety evaluation forms, incident notes, and production notices
daily production reports, wrap checklists, completion records, delivery handoff notes, and exception queues
systems_of_record: talent onboarding, time-report, and payroll-prep system
document repository for releases, permits, insurance, and guild paperwork
safety, incident, and risk-review tracking system

allowed: Classify studio-support work as accepted, ready, in-progress, blocked, rework, or completed based on named production, venue, audience, talent, permit, and wrap evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packets without claiming legal, labor, payroll, or safety signoff authority.
Require the minimum tenant facts, source records, and system retrievals needed to continue safely.
escalate: Unsigned releases, missing rights records, or other legal-clearance dependencies would change the delivery outcome.
Minor-performer evidence, labor-agreement interpretation, payroll cutoff rules, or staffing paperwork materially change the safe next action.
Permit conditions, venue rules, safety controls, engineering constraints, or special-effects approvals are missing, conflicting, or outside the lane's authority.
The governing call sheet, booking calendar, permit set, or wrap artifacts disagree on whether work is actually ready or complete.
refuse: Approve legal-rights sufficiency, chain-of-title, privacy waivers, music or likeness clearance, or other legal interpretation.
Approve payroll, labor exceptions, fire-life-safety, engineering, stunt, or special-effects authority.
Treat the industry name as cosmetic and answer without the production-specific call-sheet, venue, permit, release, audience, talent, or wrap evidence.

domain_failure_modes: Work is marked complete even though the call sheet, wrap checklist, time reports, release bundle, or daily production report does not materially support closure.
A generic event-operations response ignores production records such as stage bookings, audience manifests, Exhibit G time reports, or permit packets.
Venue-impact work proceeds without the permit, insurance, monitor, or safety dependencies that materially change whether the studio day may continue.
Minor performers or protected participants are treated as ordinary talent logistics rather than blocked until the required labor evidence is present.
The lane drifts into legal rights clearance, union interpretation, payroll approval, engineering, or fire-life-safety authority.
Audience, talent, and studio workflows are flattened into one generic process even though their records, owners, and completion evidence differ materially.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local film-office and non-California venue rules vary materially outside the California Film Commission baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require jurisdiction, venue owner, and local permit matrix retrieval before applying venue-impact decisions outside the reviewed baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Union and guild requirements vary by project type, territory, and bargaining agreement beyond the public source baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant retrieval of the governing guild or labor agreement excerpts and escalate interpretive disputes to labor-relations or production-management owners.
